author: @drhart36

If you have magnetic lines, is it then possible to use a permanent magnet alternator and generate power off the graphene sheets.  Would this be less load then copper coils?  Would this generate more energy?  Questions that I am working on.....just slow going.

author: @TheBaconWizard
text:
Right.. now I'm not a proper scientist as you know but if this is not an idiotic idea maybe someone will see it and run with it. I don't know what I'm doing, so here's the proposal. 

Say you had graphene nanoparticles suspended in a dielectric (gel?) and manipulated a magnetic field on one face of a cube made of same. That would of course attract graphene to the lines of the field. Say that field was shown in 2D on that face of a cube as a series of single tiny dots as close to each other as possible without actually joining. Could those dots of graphene effectively dope that surface/plane such that more grew on that site, effectively causing a needle of graphene to grow perpendicular to that face? A forest of them, in fact. 

It strikes me that if each such needle was 2 ions apart or more (but as little as possible) from the next, what you would have is the best capacitor energy density possible.

I have mentioned fractal patterns before and I notice someone else did recently too, but there is an issue in that what you need for this is actually a 2 ion-thick gap (I know this from one of your previous vids on the way a supercap actually works) which therefore dictates the form.

In other words, what you need is a forest of needles as I describe above. That's the best it can be. NOT a fractal, actually. 

Is this achievable? How precisely can we manipulate a magnetic field, and can we induce graphene to grow on a doped surface?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's an interesting idea - like you I am not sure how to actually implement it - especially when i think about how to implement it en masse - that is in terms of manufacturing - for that the system has to be easy to do, produce a robust product and be able to run under relatively uncontrolled conditions.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Leonard Janus interesting mate cheers

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I know - scary isn't it lol

author: @keeganmaloney3440
text:
Time to remake an antenna! My suggestion is, use a fractal electromagnetic field with a decent strength so as to form fractal structures both large and small. This could perhaps form regular structures as small as tens of nanometers across. I am quite sure at least, that you will be making them much more resonant than before.

To be clear, by fractal field, I am referring to it's frequency.

replies.author: @keeganmaloney3440
replies.text: also, in any application where you are trying to increase your surface area, it would seem that using the fractal input would be the better way to go. Admittedly, this requires the use of an electromagnetic field or very nicely made custom made magnets that I wouldn't even know how to make.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Keegan Maloney
lol - mate - the best first steps are not the complicated ones - keep it simple first then go from there - otherwise you spend all your time dreaming up scenarios that might work and doing - well - very little

replies.author: @keeganmaloney3440
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith  that doesn't even sound hard man. if you don't want to explore it, I certainly will.

I feel like I've given you gold and you said "No thanks, water is more important."

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Keegan Maloney You want me to do the work? - I didn't get that - i got you were sharing your ideas

replies.author: @keeganmaloney3440
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith im not asking you to do anything. I just figured it was worth doing. im fine with doing it myself, ill get around to it. forget i said anything.

author: @joeglory425
text: You've probably already thought of this but I'll mention it anyway.  For the purpose of optimizing output in a given surface area device, you could apply the SWAG just like you've done, let it dry, then shift the plastic sheet slightly on the magnet, and apply a second coat of SWAG such that the areas that previously had a light coat now have a thick coat.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's a surface area effect mate - an idea might be to mix in fumed silica and thereby increase surface area on a nanoscale - I don't think building up thick layers is going to help much for the SWAG - but your idea is good for an antenna i think

replies.author: @joeglory425
replies.text: Hmmm, I was interpreting the light areas as areas with little to no graphene and therefore not contributing to power output. I was thinking this method could be used to fill them in and turn them into active areas.

author: @actudoran
text:
groovy stuff !!! it comes in at a good time too ;)

you're a STAR !! :)

was thinking of antennae made out of the ink dried under the effect of the mag field and disposed one behind the other so that the would form an equidistant matrix of lines, then you would be able to collect various freq and also vector them.

Good idea about the diodes but didn't quite get the mechanism behind ... so you got a conductor that you optimise for a certain direction, but ... one way only ? using 2 sheets at 90 deg, or just one sheet ?

btw .. sorry for drifting from SWAG ...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: No worries mate - i understand - it is exciting stuff with a whole host of applications and it can sometimes be a problem to know where to post ideas - I think you are 100% right about the antenna applications and the possibility of using this to collect solar via wave transformation - one problem there is diode speed for rectification - which is why i mentioned MIM diodes in a previous vid. But antenna collection offers the possibility of 100% - or near enough - conversation - it will knock conventional cells out of the park and that is a big deal! -it's next on the list by the way lol
